claim: On October 28, 2016, FBI Director James Comey notified members of Congress that, in the course of an unrelated criminal investigation into former Congressman Anthony Weiner, the Bureau had recovered a laptop containing approximately 141,000 emails potentially relevant to its prior investigation of Hillary Clinton's use of a private email server. Comey announced the review complete and recommended no charges on November 6, 2016 — two days before the presidential election. The Department of Justice Office of the Inspector General's June 2018 report (the Horowitz Report) documented the timeline in detail. From late 2016 onward, a separate online narrative — known as “Frazzledrip” — alleged that the laptop also contained a video file showing Hillary Clinton and Huma Abedin committing satanic-ritual abuse of a child, and that nine of twelve NYPD officers who viewed it later died by suicide. The “9 of 12 officers” claim is not supported by the public record on two grounds. First, NYPD personnel did not process the laptop's contents directly: the device was transferred to the FBI for forensic review, and the contents the FBI catalogued (per the Horowitz Report) were emails, not video files. Second, the cluster of NYPD officer suicides routinely cited as corroboration occurred in 2019 — three years after the laptop was seized — during a documented NYPD mental-health crisis that year (nine officer suicides in the Department in 2019 against approximately ninety-one law-enforcement suicides nationwide per the tracking nonprofit Blue H.E.L.P.). Commissioner James O'Neill publicly described it as a mental-health crisis at the time. No primary evidence of any “Frazzledrip” video file has ever been produced. FactCheck.org and other fact-checking organizations have categorized the claim as false.

sources.label: Federal Bureau of Investigation — “FBI Communication on Discovery of Hillary Clinton E-mails on Anthony Weiner's Laptop Computer” (FBI Records Vault, multi-part FOIA release).

U.S. Department of Justice, Office of the Inspector General — “A Review of Various Actions by the Federal Bureau of Investigation and Department of Justice in Advance of the 2016 Election” (June 2018), the Horowitz Report.
FactCheck.org — “Clinton's Emails, Weiner's Laptop, and a Falsehood” (August 24, 2018), summarizing the Horowitz Report findings.
sources.quote: On Oct. 28, 2016, Comey told lawmakers in a letter that the FBI had “learned of the existence of emails that appear to be pertinent to the investigation”

sources.label: NBC News — “'Mental health crisis': Fifth NYPD officer dies by suicide in two months” (June 26, 2019), documenting the 2019 cluster cited by the conspiracy narrative.
Blue H.E.L.P. — annual law-enforcement suicide tracking, used by NYPD and federal mental-health programs.

notes: The laptop-discovery and Comey-letter facts are entirely on the public record and load-bearing for any account of the 2016 election. The 'Frazzledrip' allegation is an entirely separate online narrative with no primary evidence; it is documented here so the record reflects both the verifiable thread (Weiner laptop, Clinton email-server review) and the unverified thread that downstream actors have attached to it.
